Title: v0.2.1 — Custom themes, single-column shell, honest splash
Summary: Custom color themes (fork-a-built-in editor with 7 fields), shell migrated from horizontal nav to a single-column sidebar, splash rewired to two real checkpoints, in-app Help authoring removed.
Body:
## Headlines

### Custom color themes

The Color theme picker in [Preferences](/preferences) is no longer just a list of eight built-ins. You can fork any built-in into a **custom theme** with the **+ New** button, then tweak it in an inline 7-row editor: five palette ladders (accent, neutral, source-NPC, source-Terminal, choice — each picked color becomes the 500 stop, the other 10 stops derive at render time via CSS `color-mix`) and two single-color pickers (canvas background, canvas pattern). Each row is pre-populated from the theme's currently effective color, so you see what you're forking before you change it. Per-row ↺ buttons clear individual overrides; **Edit** on a built-in auto-forks; **Delete** rewires referencing global themes back to the base built-in. Custom themes can be referenced from multiple global themes (same colors, different fonts).

See [Themes and global themes](/help/editor-basics/themes) for the full picker tour.

### Single-column sidebar shell

The app shell migrated from a horizontal top navbar to a vertical sidebar at `w-64` on the left edge. One column carries everything that isn't page content: BLEEPFORGE branding + version, the four meta-action icons (Diagnostics / Preferences / Help / Restart), the Ctrl+K search input, then the 11 domain nav links — each with a hand-drawn pixel-art icon (house for concept, scroll for quests, robot head for NPCs, scanline-framed square for shaders, picture frame for assets, etc.).

Why: at 11 domains the horizontal navbar had outgrown its real estate, and the dialog graph view needed every vertical pixel. The active nav row gets a 4px emerald left-edge accent + bg-tint; inactive rows keep a transparent border so labels don't shift. AppSearch's dropdown anchors `left-0` so the 384px palette grows rightward over the main content area.

Popout windows (Diagnostics / Help / Preferences) skip the sidebar entirely — they're focused subviews.

### Honest 2-checkpoint splash

The splash bar used to wait for 3 checkpoints (server / preferences / catalog). Catalog turned out to fan out to 10 parallel list-fetches behind `Promise.all`, and any single slow one would pin the bar at 67% on cold boot — even though the app was perfectly usable after the user smashed CONTINUE. v0.2.1 dropped catalog from the boot gate: now it's two real checkpoints (server health + preferences/theme apply), the bar reads 50% / 100% honestly, and AppSearch's palette shows a brief "Loading…" for the half-second after dismiss while the catalog's actually populating in the background.

A randomly-picked **READY** message (`ALL SYSTEMS ONLINE`, `READY TO FORGE`, etc.) now sticks above the CONTINUE button until you click. Without it, on fast hardware the bar went 0 → 100 too fast for the per-phase flavor lines to register and the splash felt like a flicker rather than an event.

### In-app Help authoring removed

The `BLEEPFORGE_DEV_MODE` env-var gate plus the CategoryEdit / EntryEdit pages were never used in practice — Help content is edited by hand in the JSON files under `data/help/` instead. The whole authoring path came out: server middleware + PUT/DELETE routes + storage write fns + the `useDevMode` hook + helpApi write methods + the four `/help/*` edit routes + the edit buttons in the view pages + the `dispatchHelpChanged` event + the `devMode` field on `/api/health`. Net **−842 LOC** across 21 files.

Help itself is unchanged at runtime: same library, same view pages, same in-page search, same AppSearch entry. Just no in-app editor. The seed library still ships inside the AppImage and gets seeded into userData on first launch + merged on upgrade.

## Polish + bug fixes

- **Theme editor pickers populated from current values** — earlier versions of the editor opened with empty pickers ("Pick a color") which offered no anchor. Now each row reads the theme's currently effective color via canvas-pixel readback (`readVarAsHex`), so you see what you're about to override.
- **Theme editor seven fields** — earlier prototype only exposed three (accent, neutral, canvas bg). v0.2.1 expanded to cover every variable Bleepforge actually uses.
- **Sidebar meta-icons row** — four icons spaced via `justify-evenly` across the column, matching the Sidebar's other row spacings.
- **11 pixel-art nav icons** — house for concept, shield for factions, robot head for NPCs, scroll for quests, balance scale for karma, speech bubble for dialogs, smaller bubble with `…` for balloons, X-braced crate for items, scanline-framed square for shaders, open book for codex, picture frame with sun + mountain for assets.
- **Help library seeding now propagates content updates** — the seed merge tracked at `<userData>/.bleepforge-seed-manifest.json` knows what the user has edited vs. what's still untouched. Untouched files get refreshed when the seed ships an update; user-edited files are preserved.
- **Reverse-sync CLI** — `pnpm sync:from-userdata` and `pnpm sync:to-userdata` ferry the Bleepforge-only authored content (concept, codex, help, dialog `_layout.json`, shader `_meta.json`) between the AppImage's userData and the repo. Daily workflow: edit in the AppImage → `pnpm sync:from-userdata` → commit → ship.
- **Shader card color override** — every shader card now optionally carries a user-picked color (one of nine palette colors, plus an Auto fallback to the shader_type tint). Picker lives next to the pattern picker in NewShaderModal and the Edit sidebar. Persisted alongside the pattern in `data/shaders/_meta.json`.

## Under the hood

- **Custom theme runtime** — five per-palette scratch CSS vars (`--__bf-base-accent` / `-neutral` / `-source-npc` / `-source-terminal` / `-choice`) anchor the `color-mix(in oklch, white|black, picked)` expressions for each ladder stop. Browser does the math at render time; no JS color conversion.
- **Color readback for picker pre-population** — `readVarAsHex` paints any CSS color string (oklch, color-mix, named, hex) onto a 1×1 `<canvas>` and reads the sRGB pixel as `#RRGGBB`. The browser is the color parser — works for every CSS color the browser understands.
- **Cross-window theme sync** stays via the existing `bleepforge:preferences` BroadcastChannel.

## Known issues / deferred

- macOS (`.dmg`) + Windows (`.exe` / NSIS) packaging targets are still config-only follow-ups.
- Auto-update + code signing still deferred until distribution is something other than "the user runs the AppImage from disk."
- **Genericize for any Godot project** — same status as v0.2.0. ProjectIndex made discovery project-agnostic; the seven game-domain schemas + per-domain edit forms + per-domain `.tres` mappers are still hardcoded to Flock of Bleeps. Path forward: runtime-configurable schema layer, ideally driven by the project's `[GlobalClass]` resource types.

> tip: see [v0.2.0](/help/release-notes/v0-2-0) for the shaders surface + ProjectIndex foundations this release builds on.
